nodes.context: International outlaw biker gang involved in drug trafficking, contract killing, extortion, and other crimes in the UK.
Chapter of Hells Angels based in San Francisco that sponsored the East London chapter.
Chapter of Hells Angels based in Oakland that sponsored the South London chapter.
First HAMC charter in Europe, sanctioned by San Francisco Hells Angels.
First HAMC charter in Europe, sponsored by Oakland Hells Angels.
Chapter of Hells Angels in Kent, described as 'loudest' with a fortress-like clubhouse.
Chapter of Hells Angels in Windsor, involved in terrorizing a family in 1985.
Chapter of Hells Angels in Reading, involved in burning a tattoo off a man.
Chapter of Hells Angels in Sussex, whose president was cleared of assault in 2019.
Chapter of Hells Angels in Sunderland, involved in counter protests in 2020.
Chapter of Hells Angels in Manchester, whose clubhouse was attacked in 2020.
International outlaw biker gang involved in drug trafficking, contract killing, extortion, and other crimes in the UK.
Unsanctioned club that wore replicas of Hells Angels colors; later officially became Hells Angels in 1985.
Unsanctioned motorcycle gang using Hells Angels name, formed in Surrey in 1969; renamed Vikings in 1975.
Formerly Hells Angels Vikings, renamed in 1975; continued using death's head logo; rival of Hells Angels.
Support club affiliated with Hells Angels; involved in ambush on Vikings and Wargs Brotherhood in 2018.
Support group for Vikings MC; attacked by Hells Angels and Red Devils in 2018.
Hooligan firm involved in a mass brawl with Hells Angels' Lea Valley chapter in 1990.
Motorcycle gang involved in turf war with Hells Angels' English Nomads chapter in early 1990s.
Derby-based motorcycle gang; founded Rock and Blues Custom show; merged into Midlands Outlaws.
Birmingham-based biker gang; feuded with Wolverhampton Hells Angels; merged into Midlands Outlaws.
Coalition formed in 1992 against Hells Angels, consisting of Cycle Tramps, Road Tramps, and other clubs; later patched over to Outlaws MC.
American outlaw motorcycle club; Hells Angels' biggest rival internationally; established UK chapters in 2000.
London and East Anglia based biker gang; waged war with Hells Angels in late 1990s; disbanded and joined Outlaws.
Small Hertfordshire club; dispute over its absorption sparked war between Hells Angels and Outcasts.
International outlaw biker gang involved in drug trafficking, contract killing, extortion, and other crimes in the UK.
Canadian chapter of Hells Angels involved in shipping cocaine to London.
British chapter of Hells Angels that received cocaine from Montreal.
Italian-Canadian organized crime family involved in cocaine smuggling scheme with Hells Angels and Cali Cartel.
Colombian drug cartel involved in cocaine smuggling scheme with Hells Angels and Rizzuto crime family.
South African chapter of Hells Angels that exported cannabis to the UK.
British chapter of Hells Angels that opened a puppet club called the Patriots.
Puppet club of Hells Angels Kent chapter, open to current or former servicemen, suspected of stealing weapons.
Earlier Hells Angels puppet club founded in the 1980s, open to British Army members, later renamed to Patriots.
Rival outlaw biker gang involved in a brawl with Hells Angels at Birmingham Airport.

edges.evidence_quote: the first two HAMC charters in Europe were issued; the East London chapter was sanctioned by the San Francisco Hells Angels
edges.context: San Francisco Hells Angels sanctioned the East London chapter, establishing it as a charter.

edges.evidence_quote: the South London chapter was sponsored by the Hells Angels in Oakland
edges.context: Oakland Hells Angels sponsored the South London chapter, establishing it as a charter.

edges.evidence_quote: Both London chapters merged in 1973.
edges.context: The East London and South London chapters merged into a single entity.

edges.evidence_quote: On 15 April 1979, a group of up to thirty Hells Angels armed with guns, axes and knives ambushed fifteen members of the rogue Windsor Hells Angels who were sleeping in a car park near Brockenhurst.
edges.context: Hells Angels attacked the unsanctioned Windsor Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels Vikings were 'in a constant state of war' with the official Hells Angels chapter in London and were renamed the Vikings in 1975.
edges.context: Hells Angels Vikings were in a state of war with official Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: Hells Angels member David Wyeth, along with an accomplice, carried out an assault and attempted to steal the 'colours' of a Vikings member in Maidstone on 7 May 2018.
edges.context: Hells Angels attacked a Vikings member in 2018.

edges.evidence_quote: On 7 November 2018, members of the Hells Angels' Slough chapter and the affiliated Red Devils ambushed six members of the Vikings and their support group the Wargs Brotherhood.
edges.context: Red Devils are affiliated with Hells Angels and jointly attacked Vikings and Wargs.

edges.evidence_quote: On 7 November 2018, members of the Hells Angels' Slough chapter and the affiliated Red Devils ambushed six members of the Vikings and their support group the Wargs Brotherhood.
edges.context: Hells Angels attacked Wargs Brotherhood, a support group for Vikings.

edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Hells Angels' Lea Valley chapter took part in a mass brawl against a group from the Luton Town MIGs hooligan firm at the Blockers Arms public house in Luton in May 1990.
edges.context: Hells Angels fought with Luton Town MIGs in a brawl.

edges.evidence_quote: The Nomads chapter became involved in a turf war with the Southampton chapter of the Satans Slaves, which began after Hells Angels members Stephen Harris and Barry Burn were fired upon during a trip to Bristol.
edges.context: Hells Angels were in a turf war with Satans Slaves.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolverhampton Hells Angels chapter became involved in a longrunning dispute with the Derby-based Road Tramps motorcycle gang over control of the 'Rock and Blues Custom show'.
edges.context: Hells Angels disputed with Road Tramps over a motorcycle festival.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolverhampton Hells Angels were simultaneously involved in a feud with the Cycle Tramps biker gang of Birmingham, which resulted in dozens of assaults, numerous stabbings and shootings, and several mass armed brawls in the early 1990s.
edges.context: Hells Angels feuded with Cycle Tramps, leading to violence.

edges.evidence_quote: The various gangs united to become the Midlands Outlaws, whose 'colours' consisted of a skull wearing Native American headdress featuring seven different coloured feathers, with each colour representing one of the founding clubs.
edges.context: Midlands Outlaws formed as a coalition against Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: The Midlands Outlaws later began 'prospecting' for the American Outlaws Association — the Hells Angels' biggest rival internationally — and 'patched over' to join the A.O.A. in February 2000.
edges.context: Midlands Outlaws patched over to become part of Outlaws MC.

edges.evidence_quote: During the late 1990s, the Hells Angels waged a two-year war against the Outcasts biker gang, which was centred in London and East Anglia.
edges.context: Hells Angels waged war against Outcasts.

edges.evidence_quote: The dispute began when the Outcasts tried to absorb a small Hertfordshire club, the Lost Tribe, in June 1997. Concerned that such a move would make the Outcasts their equal in numbers, the Hells Angels themselves then laid claim to the Lost Tribe.
edges.context: Hells Angels claimed Lost Tribe to prevent Outcasts from absorbing it.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outcasts disbanded as a result of the conflict, aligning themselves with the Midlands Outlaws and joining the February 2000 'patch over' to the American Outlaws Association.
edges.context: Outcasts aligned with Outlaws after disbanding.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws, who are mainly based in the West Midlands, have since become the Hells Angels' main rivals in the United Kingdom since opening chapters in the country in February 2000.
edges.context: Outlaws are the main rivals of Hells Angels in the UK.

edges.evidence_quote: The British Hells Angels have a close association with the Canadian Hells Angels, and the Angels' Montreal chapter was involved in shipping cocaine to the Angels' London chapter.
edges.context: Montreal chapter shipped cocaine to London chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: Pierre Rodrigue and David Rouleau, two Canadian Hells Angels from the Sherbrooke chapter, were arrested by British police in London at the request of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) in February 1995 before being extradited to Canada and sentenced to fourteen years' imprisonment for conspiring to smuggle 558 kilograms of cocaine into the UK in a scheme also involving the Rizzuto crime family and the Cali Cartel.
edges.context: Montreal Hells Angels conspired with Rizzuto crime family and Cali Cartel to smuggle cocaine.

edges.evidence_quote: Pierre Rodrigue and David Rouleau, two Canadian Hells Angels from the Sherbrooke chapter, were arrested by British police in London at the request of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) in February 1995 before being extradited to Canada and sentenced to fourteen years' imprisonment for conspiring to smuggle 558 kilograms of cocaine into the UK in a scheme also involving the Rizzuto crime family and the Cali Cartel.
edges.context: Montreal Hells Angels conspired with Cali Cartel and Rizzuto crime family to smuggle cocaine.

edges.evidence_quote: A South African Hells Angel had been arrested in Swaziland for seeking to buy marihuana on a massive scale as the Johannesburg chapter had received an order from the British Hells Angels for marihuana.
edges.context: Johannesburg chapter exported cannabis to British Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kent chapter of the Hells Angels opened up a puppet club in 1997 called the Patriots that was only open to current or former servicemen.
edges.context: Kent chapter created the Patriots as a puppet club.

edges.evidence_quote: The Patriots had its origins in another Hells Angels puppet club founded in the 1980s called Soldier Blue that was only open to members of the British Army.
edges.context: Patriots originated from Soldier Blue, which was renamed.

edges.evidence_quote: He would later be one of the three Hells Angels jailed for six years after the brawl with the Outlaws at Birmingham Airport.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Outlaws engaged in a brawl at Birmingham Airport.
